perturbation.original_text: The term "Confidential Inform ation" shall mean and include that technical and non- technical information disclosed by CIF to Dealer relating to: (a) CIF's website, information system, and related software and discs, and any data and information contained therein, (b) information and specifications on CIF's products, and their design, cost, manufacture, marketing, and sale, and (c) the existence, terms and subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Confidential Information" shall mean and include technical information disclosed by CIF to Dealer relating to: (a) CIF's website. (b) Information on CIF's products.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'non-technical information', 'information system, and related software and discs, and any data and information contained therein', 'specifications on CIF's products, and their design, cost, manufacture, marketing, and sale' and 'the existence, terms and subject matter of this Agreement' from the definition of "Confidential Information" in Section 1, the contract creates an internal contradiction. Later sections, such as Section 4 that discusses the ownership/return of confidential information, now lack clarity on whether non-technical data, designs, costs, etc., are still protected. This creates uncertainty regarding what information the Dealer is obligated to return and what CIF retains ownership of, creating an in-text contradiction when referencing back to this term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Dealer agrees and acknowledges that the Confidential Information is the property of CIF, and upon termination of this Agreement all rights to the use and ownership of the Confidential Inform ation shall be retained exclusively by CIF. In the event that CIF requests that Dealer return any of its Confidential Information, Dealer agrees to return all of the Confidential Information delivered to it, and each copy or duplicate thereof, within thirty (30) days of the date of the request thereof.
perturbation.justification: The modified text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' by omitting key elements present in the original definition, such as 'non-technical information,' details about CIF's systems, and specifics regarding product design and marketing. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that 'all rights to the use and ownership of the Confidential Information shall be retained exclusively by CIF' and requires the Dealer to return 'all of the Confidential Information.' The modified definition creates ambiguity about what constitutes 'Confidential Information,' making it unclear what the Dealer is obligated to return and what CIF retains ownership of, thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Dealer shall not distribute, disseminate, publish or disclose the Confidential Information to any person, or entity not employed or retained by Dealer or the use of the Confidential Information for any purpose other than to further the marketing and sale of CIF products. Dealer will disclose the Confidential Information only to those individuals employed by Dealer who need to know such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Dealer shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any entity not employed by Dealer. Dealer will disclose the Confidential Information only to individuals employed by Dealer.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrases 'distribute, disseminate, publish', 'or retained by Dealer or the use of the Confidential Information for any purpose other than to further the marketing and sale of CIF products' and 'who need to know such Confidential Information' from Section 2, the contract introduces ambiguity. The omission of 'disseminate, publish' implies that the Dealer is still allowed to disseminate or publish Confidential Information. The omission of 'or retained by Dealer' is not being able to disclose to any person retained by the dealer such as lawyers, accountants, etc, because they are not employed by them. The omission of the purpose that should only be used, creates ambiguity when reffering back to section 1 with the term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Confidential Information" shall mean and include technical information disclosed by CIF to Dealer relating to: (a) CIF's website. (b) Information on CIF's products.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the scope of permissible disclosures. The original text allowed disclosure to individuals 'retained by Dealer' and for purposes 'to further the marketing and sale of CIF products', as well as only to those 'who need to know'. The changed text eliminates these exceptions, creating a contradiction. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information', and the modified text restricts how that information can be shared, directly impacting the utility of the defined term. The omission of these elements introduces ambiguity and restricts uses that might have been permissible under the original contract, leading to a strong in-text contradiction because the dealer cannot disclose information to retained individuals or use it for the defined purpose even if related to CIF products, and the reason to why employees need to know the information.

perturbation.original_text: Term . The parties agree that the foregoing obligations of confidentiality and non- disclosure shall terminate five (5) years from the Effective Date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Term . The parties agree that the obligations of confidentiality shall terminate upon delivery of product.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of the Term clause introduces a contradiction. The original clause specifies a five-year term for confidentiality obligations. By changing it to termination upon 'delivery of product', the contract creates ambiguity about the duration of confidentiality. If CIF provides a product design, does confidentiality end when the design is delivered? This contradicts the general premise of protecting CIF's confidential information over a defined period, thus creating an in-text contradiction with the rest of the agreement's assumptions and intent. The term "Effective Date" is nowhere to be found after this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement supersedes all prior and contemporaneous negotiations, agreements, discussions and writings and constitutes the entire Agreement between the parties with respect to subject matter hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a term of five years for confidentiality, aligning with the intent of protecting information for a defined period. The changed text replaces this with 'upon delivery of product,' creating a direct conflict. The contradicted text states the agreement is the 'entire Agreement between the parties with respect to subject matter hereof,' implying a comprehensive and consistent framework. The modified 'Term' clause undermines this comprehensiveness by introducing ambiguity and directly contradicting the established expectation of a fixed confidentiality term, thus creating an in-text contradiction. This is because the term 'delivery of product' is nowhere mentioned or agreed upon as a term for confidentiality. Thus the new agreement isn't entire.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that CIF requests that Dealer return any of its Confidential Information, Dealer agrees to return all of the Confidential Information delivered to it, and each copy or duplicate thereof, within thirty (30) days of the date of the request thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that CIF requests that Dealer return Confidential Information, Dealer agrees to return the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'any of its', 'all of the Confidential Information delivered to it, and each copy or duplicate thereof' and 'within thirty (30) days of the date of the request thereof', the contract creates an internal contradiction regarding the specifics of returning Confidential Information. Omitting 'any of its' and 'all of the Confidential Information delivered to it, and each copy or duplicate thereof' introduces ambiguity about what exactly needs to be returned. It contradicts the goal of complete return as stated in the original paragraph, leaving room for interpretation. Furthermore, the omission of 'within thirty (30) days of the date of the request thereof' leaves uncertainty about the timeframe, directly contradicting the original explicit deadline.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Confidential Information" shall mean and include technical information disclosed by CIF to Dealer relating to: (a) CIF's website. (b) Information on CIF's products.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit requirement to return 'all' Confidential Information and 'each copy or duplicate thereof', and the deadline of 'within thirty (30) days of the date of the request thereof', which creates a strong contradiction. The contradicted text specifies that 'Confidential Information' includes technical information disclosed by CIF to Dealer relating to CIF's website and products. By removing 'all' and 'each copy,' the changed text introduces ambiguity about whether partial returns are acceptable, directly conflicting with the original intent and creating a potential inconsistency. Further, removing the 30-day deadline creates a direct conflict since the original contract specified a timeframe, and the modified contract does not. This creates a strong contradiction as obligations are not consistently defined within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Any notice required or permitted u nder this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered and deemed received as follows: (i) by personal delivery or by facsimile (with electronic confirmation) during the recipient’s normal business hours, in which case it shall be deemed received at the time of such delivery or facsimile as the case may be; (ii) by certified or registered mail, in which case it shall be deemed received on the date such return receipt is signed; or (iii) by prepaid nationally recognized overnight courier by next day delivery, in which case it shall be deemed received on the day following the date of deposit with such courier.
perturbation.changed_text: Any notice required under this Agreement shall be in writing.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the detailed methods of notice (personal delivery, fax, mail, courier) the contract now only specifies that notice must be in writing. This directly contradicts the need for a defined, reliable way to deliver official communication. Consider a scenario where CIF attempts to enforce the return of Confidential Information. With the detailed notice provisions removed, CIF is now only required to send 'written' notice, opening to uncertainty of which method to use. This contradicts other sections that implicitly rely on the old notice to inform about confidential information and any other relevant request.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that CIF requests that Dealer return Confidential Information, Dealer agrees to return the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined specific methods for delivering notice (personal delivery, fax, mail, courier), while the changed text only requires notice to be in writing, removing the details about delivery methods. This omission creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that 'CIF requests that Dealer return Confidential Information, Dealer agrees to return the Confidential Information.' Without a defined delivery method as previously established, it becomes unclear how CIF can reliably request the return of confidential information, making enforcement problematic. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon and shal l inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns. Dealer shall not assign this Agreement without the express written permission of CIF.Expect as otherwise provided herein, nothing in this Agreement, express or implied, is intended to confer on any person other than the parties hereto or their respective successors and permitted assigns any rights, remedies, obligations, or liabilities under or by reason of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon the parties hereto. Dealer shall not assign this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and shall inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns', 'without the express written permission of CIF' and 'Expect as otherwise provided herein, nothing in this Agreement, express or implied, is intended to confer on any person other than the parties hereto or their respective successors and permitted assigns any rights, remedies, obligations, or liabilities under or by reason of this Agreement.', this section contradicts sections like 5(j) enforcement since it doesn't allow successors and permitted assigns to be bound under the agreement, thus it is creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Should any legal proceedings be commenced to secure or enforce any right under this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover from the non-prevailing party its reasonable attorneys' fees and costs, in addition to all other relief to which the prevailing party may be entitled at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes language that allows the agreement to benefit successors and permitted assigns. The contradicted text, specifically the enforcement clause, implies that rights under the agreement can be secured or enforced. By removing the language about successors and assigns, the changed text creates a contradiction because successors and assigns would no longer be able to enforce rights under the agreement, thus creating an in-text contradiction as described in the explanation and the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition.
